recentArticles.title: Every Record Justin Bieber and SKYLRK Broke Before Coachella Weekend 2 - Newsweek
recentArticles.snippet: # Every Record Justin Bieber and SKYLRK Broke Before Coachella Weekend 2. While the true triumph was that Bieber staged an evocative, genuine set, the hard data backs up that he and his SKYLRK brand also pulled off the most successful Coachella takeover by every available measure. Various outlets have reported that Bieber's reported $10 million for headlining Coachella is the richest earnings of any artist in the festival's history. Bieber also generated the highest ticket demand and the priciest tickets sold, while his 90-minute performance became the most viewed in Coachella history. Bieber and Neima Khaila co-founded SKYLRK, which officially launched last July. Bieber wore nothing but blue silk SKYLRK boxers and black SKYLRK socks while performing a stripped-back version of "YUKON" at the 2026 Grammys on Feb. 1, and he sported a SKYLRK outfit on the Coachella stage last weekend. ## The MAGA Trump Divorce, Six Months Later | Opinion.

recentArticles.title: TENILLE TOWNES RELEASES INTROSPECTIVE, SELF-PRODUCED NEW ALBUM THE ACROBAT - rocknloadmag.com
recentArticles.snippet: # Tenille Townes Releases Introspective, Self-Produced New Album ‘The Acrobat’. **TENILLE TOWNES****RELEASES INTROSPECTIVE, SELF-PRODUCED NEW ALBUM*****THE ACROBAT***. Friday, April 10 – Today, acclaimed singer-songwriter **Tenille Townes** released her highly anticipated new album ***The Acrobat***. The song also gained support from media with Holler describing the tune as *“a quietly devastating song that confronts the emotional cost of loving someone at the expense of yourself.”* PopMatterspraised “we could use a little more,” stating how the track’s “*uncompromising quietude demonstrates how loud the world has become”*and on title track “the acrobat” featuring Lori McKenna*,*Music Row applauded the *“starkly compelling artistry where you hang on every line.”*No Depressionhas already dubbed the record *“a terrific act of creative transformation.”*. Whether on record or on stage, Townes continues to create what she calls a “safe space” for listeners.Her live shows balance moments of quiet reflection with joy and release, grounded in the belief that music can help people feel less alone.*“The greatest measure of success for me is the stories people share about how a song helped them,”*she says.

recentArticles.title: Isaia Huron confronts love, projection, and self-definition on ‘Mr. Lovebomb’ [Interview] - EARMILK
recentArticles.snippet: Alternative R&B, soul, and pop singer, songwriter, and producer **Isaia Huron** has released his new record ***Mr. Lovebomb***, a title that feels as much like a societal critique as it does a personal moniker, reclaiming and owning a term often loaded with projection. More on his new record, Huron talks about how there was a particular song that came together early on that helped define the direction of *Mr. Lovebomb*: “The first song I made was “Side Slider," which is obviously the last song, but I needed to try to build a story that led up to this point of the main character having a moment of clarity of why he feels justified to cheat on his girl through the lens of uncovering why he's broken. Huron continues: ‘These guys have made it so easy for me to produce a record that, in essence, feels cohesive sonically and makes way for the story to shine through.

Freshman Calista Chou has found a home in her varsity volleyball team. I had a really fun time being able to play with my team at a high level,” she said. “I am very happy to be a freshman on varsity, and it helped me boost my confidence,” he said. “Being on varsity has helped me to talk with upperclassmen and make friends with more people who are older than me,” he said.

recentArticles.title: Justin Bieber headlines Coachella with nostalgia-fuelled set - BBC
recentArticles.snippet: # Justin Bieber headlines Coachella with nostalgia-fuelled set. Justin Bieber made a nostalgia-fuelled return to the major stage as Coachella's much-anticipated headline act. It marked the star's biggest live show in four years, having cancelled his Justice world tour following health issues. Dressed in a hoodie and shorts on a stripped-back stage, Bieber was joined by musical guests including The Kid Laroi, Wizkid, Tems and Dijon. The star's wife Hailey Bieber was spotted in the crowd, alongside celebrity couple Timothée Chalamet and Kylie Jenner. Bieber cancelled his world tour after revealing he was suffering from facial paralysis, after being diagnosed with Ramsay Hunt syndrome - a complication from contracting shingles - in 2022. His Coachella set marked a starker departure from the previous night's headline act, Sabrina Carpenter, whose Hollywood-themed gig included costume changes, choreography and elaborate stage design. ## Bieber cancels remaining Justice world tour dates. ## Justin and Hailey Bieber announce birth of first child.

recentArticles.title: Jin: ‘My Biggest Dream Has Been to Go on Tour With BTS’ - Rolling Stone
recentArticles.snippet: In our cover-story interview, BTS’ powerhouse singer talks about his rock-influenced solo work, loving Coldplay, and what really matters to him. Even in a remarkably fan-centered group, his attentiveness to the feelings of ARMY is intense — he saw his solo EPs and shows primarily as a way to keep fans entertained during the group’s break, and he pushed to extend BTS’ tour. He’s also the group’s designated rocker, even collaborating with Coldplay, though he hasn’t had time to see as many bands as he’d like. I tend to like rock-adjacent genres, and have always wanted to include that sound in a solo album. **Your solo tour had rock energy and a new approach — it was very different from what you do with BTS. How did that experience carry forward into both making the album and when you’re going to be touring with BTS?**. I feel like it takes those rock elements that you love and that you use so well.**.

recentArticles.snippet: # Durand Bernarr Announces Self-Titled Album ‘BERNARR’. Listen to recently-released single 'Wild Ride'. **Durand Bernarr** will release his fourth studio album, ‘BERNARR’, on May 1st. The album features collaborators from the RnB and modern soul continuum, including James Fauntleroy – who features on the nocturnal prelude single ‘Wild Ride’ – Donnie Scantz, Bryan‑Michael Cox, Johntá Austin, Kandi Burrus, Khalid, Raphael Saadiq, Sevyn Streeter, Troy Taylor, Vic Mensa and more. Bernarr earned his first GRAMMY for Best Progressive R&B Album, for his 2025 release ‘**BLOOM**’. Known for his genre-bending sound and theatrical command, Bernarr blends classic soul tradition with forward looking artistry, drawing comparisons to Luther Vandross, Little Richard, and Rahsaan Patterson. * + News / Robin Murray  / 15 · 04 · 2026. * + News / Shahzaib Hussain  / 14 · 04 · 2026. * + News / Robin Murray  / 14 · 04 · 2026. * + News / Shahzaib Hussain  / 14 · 04 · 2026.

recentArticles.title: Jason Bembry’s ‘My Friends’ Embraces Self-Confidence - clashmusic.com
recentArticles.snippet: # Jason Bembry’s ‘My Friends’ Embraces Self-Confidence. **Jason Bembry** returns with new single ‘My Friends’. **Jason Bembry** uses music as a means to explore his emotions, processing some complex feelings in the process. Out now, new single ‘My Friends’ finds Jason Bembry revelling in self-confidence. The studio gloss seems to amplify the emotional meaning of the lyrics, with the 29-year-old using the single as a means to express self-worth. Merging the introspection of **James Blake** with the dancefloor expression of **Disclosure**, ‘My Friends’ spotlights Jason Bembry’s multi-faceted musicality. *‘My Friends’ is all about the isolation and self-confidence that comes from feeling othered by people you thought were your friends. Jordan’s ‘Thomas Crown Affair’ Trailer Seduces CinemaCon With Art, Sex and an Oscar-Winning Six Pack. Fresh off a triumphant Oscar win, Michael B. Jordan hit CinemaCon to showcase his latest directorial effort: a slick remake of “The Thomas Crown Affair.”. The annual convention of movie theater owners got a sneak peek at Jordan’s project — the third big-screen take on the story of a billionaire whose idle hands lead him to orchestrate major art thefts. Distributor Amazon MGM Studios cold opened their CinemaCon presentation with the trailer, showcasing Jordan in all his glossy leading man glory. The trailer played through the roof, which you can partly attribute to perfect timing — Jordan hasn’t been seen since his incredible best actor win for “Sinners.”. That doesn’t land today,” Jordan told *Variety* in November.

recentArticles.snippet: # Watch James Taylor, Linda Ronstadt & More Laud Peter Asher in ‘Peter Asher: Everywhere Man’ Documentary Trailer. He’s “the legend behind music’s biggest legends,” as Asher is dubbed in the trailer, premiering below, for *Peter Asher: Everywhere Man*, a documentary directed and produced by Dayna Goldfine and Dan Geller, the team also behind the 2021 film *Hallelujah: Leonard Cohen, a Journey, A Song.*. “As filmmakers who came of age listening to and revering the singers, songwriters and musicians who appear in *Peter Asher: Everywhere Man,* we thought we were relatively savvy about their narratives, but until this project we had no idea about the myriad ways in which Peter shaped and influenced not only their individual origin stories, music and careers, but also some of the now-accepted paradigms about the music industry in general,” Goldfine says.

recentArticles.snippet: * Jay is a TikToker whose posts critical of influencer behavior often go viral. That video was the start of Jay's side hustle as a prominent influencer critic. Daadi Snacks now boasts over a million TikTok followers, and while the videos aren't traditional product marketing, Jay said they've helped grow awareness of his family's brand. A TikToker known as themilehams, who has been targeted by Jay's criticism, said accounts like his fuel bullying. Jay, who often takes aim at food and travel influencers, said most feedback on his videos has been positive, and that many of his posts are based on tips from followers. The poster, an Atlanta influencer who goes by BestieBri, said in a statement to Business Insider that she didn't mean to attack the café. "It's not like people go to school for 'How to show up and be an influencer,'" he said.

recentArticles.snippet: # R&B/Hip-Hop Fresh Picks of the Week: Kenyon Dixon, Momo Boyd, Fergie Baby & More. Listen to new must-hear songs from R&B/hip-hop artists such as Casper Sage and Denise Julia. With Fresh Picks, *Billboard* aims to highlight some of the best and most interesting new sounds across R&B and hip-hop — from a steamy new Kenyon Dixon single to a standout cut from Momo Boyd’s debut solo EP. Following an extended hiatus, the Filipina singer kicked off her next era with “2GOOD4U.” The sensual R&B track boasts a throwback 2000s feel and veers into the pop lane, while Deej ruminates about moving on from a trifling ex. Fergie Baby is looking to hold down Harlem for what looks like another summer after following up his impressive *Summer League Series, Vol. 1* project last July with the uptown anthems “Good Day to Be in Harlem” alongside Ferg and now the “Harlem River Drive (Flee-mix)” with the help of Wavy Arty on the boards and Harlem legend Cam’ron.

recentArticles.title: University of Wisconsin Board of Regents votes to fire system President Jay Rothman - Wisconsin Examiner
recentArticles.snippet: # University of Wisconsin Board of Regents votes to fire system President Jay Rothman. The University of Wisconsin Board of Regents voted unanimously to fire UW President Jay Rothman on Tuesday, ending his tenure as the leader of UW system campuses. The University of Wisconsin Board of Regents voted unanimously to fire UW President Jay Rothman on Tuesday, ending his tenure as the leader of UW system campuses and launching the process of finding a new leader. In a statement, the Board of Regents said that Rothman had “worked hard to bring the best to the campuses, students, faculty and staff” and those efforts are appreciated, but “despite these accomplishments, based on the annual performance review and subsequent discussions, the Board has lost confidence in President Rothman’s ability to lead the UWs moving forward.”. WMC said Rothman has helped to enhance the UW’s reputation and “that reputation will be severely tarnished by the Board of Regents if it votes to remove Jay from his position without justification.”.
recentArticles.summary: University of Wisconsin Board of Regents votes to fire system President Jay Rothman - Wisconsin Examiner

recentArticles.title: Agriculture Security Is National Security’ — Florida Lt. Gov. Jay Collins Meets With Local Farmers - NorthEscambia.com
recentArticles.snippet: # NorthEscambia.com. * All Battery Of Escambia County Jail Inmate Leads To Arrest Of Two Corrections Officers. Jay Collins Meets With Local Farmers. * MILO, The Mobile ID And Licensing Office, Is In Walnut Hill And Century This Week. * Florida Gas Prices Decline 20 Cents, But Escambia County Trends Upward. Jay Collins Meets With Local Farmers. Jay Collins met with farmers from Escambia and Santa Rosa counties on Monday in Jay. “Having grown up on a family farm, touring Diamond Farm in Santa Rosa County today and spending time with those on the front lines of Florida agriculture was personal for me,” Collins said. Collins visited several farms in the Jay area to discuss the challenges that farmers are facing from the economy and climate. “Supporting our farmers isn’t optional; it’s essential to keeping Florida and America strong,” Collins said. **Photos for NorthEscambia.com, click to enlarge.**. recentArticles.title: 'Michael' Clip of Jaafar Jackson's 'Billie Jean' Performance Slammed by Fans: 'F--king Atrocious' - TheWrap
recentArticles.snippet: “Literally the least inspired cinematography I’ve ever seen,” one critic writes on X. A new clip from the highly anticipated Michael Jackson biopic, titled “Michael,” was released Wednesday and featured star Jaafar Jackson’s take on “Billie Jean.” And, after being viewed over 8 million times, the consensus for the sneak peek was resoundingly negative. In the clip, Jackson, the real-life nephew of the late King of Pop, can be seen wearing his uncle’s signature white glove and black bedazzled jacket while singing the 1983 hit, “Billie Jean.” The scene is a recreation of the late Jackson’s famed performance for “Motown 25: Yesterday, Today, Forever,” where he debuted his famous moonwalk move for the first time. “Literally the least inspired cinematography I’ve ever seen,” one critic wrote in response to the clip’s release. “Michael,” which is set to hit theaters on April 24, has faced its fair share of drama leading up to the release, including millions in reshoots, a scrapped final act, a delayed release and more.
recentArticles.summary: 這篇新聞報導關於麥可傑克森傳記電影預告片評價負面，與周杰倫無關。

recentArticles.title: How Following Raye’s Vision Led to a Career High Debut With ‘This Music May Contain Hope’ - Billboard
recentArticles.snippet: ### RAYE’s ‘THIS MUSIC MAY CONTAIN HOPE.’ Makes Top 10 Debut on 4 Billboard Album Charts. Since then, her trajectory has been on a big upswing: the success of *Blues* led to a Grammy nomination for best new artist in 2025, where she stole the show at the ceremony with a rendition of “Oscar Winning Tears,” and the subsequent release of her second album, *THIS MUSIC MAY CONTAIN HOPE*, and its breakout single “Where Is My Husband?” This week, *HOPE* debuted at No. 11 on the Billboard 200 and “Husband” reached No. 11 on the Hot 100, both career-high marks for Raye — feats which earn Human Re Sources founder/CEO **J Erving** the title of *Billboard*’s Executive of the Week. **This week, Raye’s new album *THIS MUSIC MAY CONTAIN HOPE* debuted in the top 10 on four different *Billboard* charts, and hit No. 11 on the Billboard 200, a career high for her.
recentArticles.summary: 此新聞報導主要關於歌手Raye新專輯在Billboard排行榜上取得的佳績，與周杰倫無直接關聯。

recentArticles.title: YouTuber Jesser Launches Parent Company, Preps Business Expansion - ADWEEK
recentArticles.snippet: # YouTuber Jesser Launches Parent Company, Preps Business Expansion. ## The media company, which generates an eight-figure revenue, has content and product debuts coming later this year. On Friday, the YouTube creator Jesse “Jesser” Riedel announced the formation of JesserCo., a new parent company that will unite his growing portfolio of businesses under a single structure. The 27-year-old Jesser will remain founder, while Zach Miller, who previously served as president of Bucketsquad, Jesser’s apparel brand, has been elevated to president of JesserCo. The reorganization brings together two main operating businesses: Jesser Media, the newly named home for his content operation, and Bucketsquad, the basketball lifestyle and apparel brand. Jesser Media reaches more than 45 million subscribers and followers, primarily through his YouTube channel, and has generated more than 10 billion views across platforms, according to the company. Mark Stenberg is ADWEEK's senior media reporter. ## Media. #### EXCLUSIVE: Soccer Media Startup Footballco Wants to Own New York's World Cup Summer.
recentArticles.summary: 此資訊與周杰倫無關，主要報導YouTuber Jesser成立母公司JesserCo.並擴展其內容和服飾品牌業務。

recentArticles.title: Why Jay McInerney got fired from the New Yorker (Fresh Air+) : Fresh Air - NPR
recentArticles.snippet: **Why Jay McInerney got fired from the New Yorker (Fresh Air+)** **Writer Jay McInerney first came onto the literary scene in the 1980s, writing about New York City, drugs, and excess in his hit novel, “Bright Lights, Big City.” He returns with a new book this month, "See You on the Other Side," completing his four-book series on a NYC couple navigating marital tensions and aging. | To access bonus episodes, sponsor-free listening and to support public radio, become a Fresh Air+ supporter at plus.npr.org/freshair.**. # Why Jay McInerney got fired from the New Yorker (Fresh Air+). #### Why Jay McInerney got fired from the New Yorker (Fresh Air+). Writer Jay McInerney first came onto the literary scene in the 1980s, writing about New York City, drugs, and excess in his hit novel, “Bright Lights, Big City.” He returns with a new book this month, "See You on the Other Side," completing his four-book series on a NYC couple navigating marital tensions and aging.

recentArticles.title: A Belieber's Review of Justin Bieber's Coachella 2026 Set - Vogue Philippines
recentArticles.snippet: Two hours before Justin Bieber began his 2026 Coachella set last night, I found myself inching closer and closer towards the main stage, where the Canadian pop star and late millennial icon was set to perform at 11:25 p.m. Some diehard Beliebers—Justin’s fiercest fans—were already camped out near the front barricades. Bieber began totally alone on the stage with one of his new tracks, “All I Can Take.” In true J.B. fashion, he emerged wearing a large oversized pink hoodie from his own brand SKYLRK, and baggy shorts by Lu’u Dan. Then came other tracks from his *Swag* and *Swag II* albums: he sang “Speed Demon,” “Go Baby,” “Everything Hallelujah”—with the latter track, the camera panned to his wife Hailey Bieber in the crowd, as she watched on lovingly and sang along. Nearing the end of the show, Bieber shed his pink hoodie and shades to rock a simple black cutoff tee—returning to perform more stripped-down *Swag* tracks like “Yukon” and “Daisies.” More special guests emerged, including Tems, Wizkid, Dijon, the Kid LAROI, and Mk.gee.
recentArticles.summary: 此新聞報導關於小賈斯汀（Justin Bieber）在2026年Coachella音樂節的演出，與周杰倫無直接關聯。

recentArticles.title: Kehlani Teams Up With Missy Elliott For Aaliyah-Tributing ‘Back and Forth’ Single - Billboard
recentArticles.snippet: It finds Kehlani in their signature lights-low R&B groove, with Elliott kicking thing off with some bold bars, boasting, “You know it’s some hot s–t/ Let me say what I’m gon’ say, let me stay on the topic/ Girl, woman, I do what I wanna do/ Why you trippin’ when I come right home to you?” over the song’s languorous beat before Kehlani steps in on the whispery first verse. The pre-chorus amps up the heat, with Kehlani singing, “And by-y-y-y-y/ By doing this later, I do us a favor / And by-y-y-y-y/ Don’t ruin my makeup, be there when you wake up,” before dipping into the singsongy chorus that pays homage to Elliott’s legendary late friend and collaborator Aaliyah’s 1994 debut hit single “Back & Forth.”. The new song comes after an epic run for Kehlani in 2025, which included the crossover anthem “Folded,” which landed the singer several chart milestones, with the track peaking at No. 6 on the Billboard Hot 100, marking her first top 10 on that singles chart.
recentArticles.summary: 此新聞資訊與周杰倫無關，主要報導Kehlani與Missy Elliott合作新歌並向Aaliyah致敬。
